study_title: Smart Inhaler Support for Uncontrolled Asthma (BREATHE-EASY)
design_summary: 16-week, single-arm study of a sensor-equipped 'smart' controller inhaler with app-based adherence coaching for adults whose asthma is not well controlled. Participants use the study app daily.

criteria.probe: To start, how old are you?
Have you been diagnosed with asthma by a doctor?
Are you prescribed a daily controller inhaler - the kind you take every day, not just when symptoms hit?
criteria.unknown_rule: If the participant cannot identify whether any of their inhalers is a daily controller, INC3 is unknown.

criteria.text: Uses a rescue (reliever) inhaler 2 or more times per week, on average, over the past month (indicating suboptimal control).
criteria.probe: How often do you use your rescue inhaler in a typical week?
criteria.unknown_rule: If the participant cannot estimate weekly rescue-inhaler use, INC4 is unknown.

criteria.text: Owns a smartphone and is willing to use the study app daily.
criteria.probe: Do you own a smartphone, and would you be willing to use our study app every day?

criteria.text: Willing to participate for the full 16 weeks.
criteria.probe: The program runs a full 16 weeks - is that a commitment you can make?

criteria.text: Diagnosed COPD, emphysema, or chronic bronchitis (including asthma-COPD overlap).
criteria.probe: Have you ever been diagnosed with COPD, emphysema, or chronic bronchitis?

criteria.text: Took an oral corticosteroid course (for example prednisone) for asthma within the past 4 weeks.
criteria.probe: Have you taken an oral steroid like prednisone for your asthma in the past four weeks?

criteria.text: Hospitalized or treated in an emergency department for asthma within the past 3 months.
criteria.probe: In the past three months, have you been to the hospital or emergency room for your asthma?

criteria.text: Currently enrolled in another interventional clinical trial or structured asthma program.
criteria.probe: Are you currently enrolled in another clinical trial or a structured asthma program?
